Elective Module
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UED3  |
VHS:
21h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30  |
Coefficient:
1  |
Credits:
1
Teaching Objectives
This module allows students to acquire a broader vision of their field of study and promotes a better understanding of the social, economic and ethical issues related to computing, as well as the ability to communicate effectively and adapt to various situations. It enables:
Opening to other disciplines related to everyday life, in order to understand the importance of collaboration in a multidisciplinary environment
Broadening of horizons, fostering an understanding of human thought across time and space
Development of transversal skills: communication, holistic vision, analysis and synthesis, adaptability, etc.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to the relevant discipline (chosen subject)
Presentation of fundamental concepts
Cross-applications with computer science
Case studies
Possible Elective Modules
Green computing
Digital governance and transformation
Philosophy of science and technology
Digital law and data protection (GDPR)
Cognitive psychology
Emerging technologies (Blockchain, IoT, …)
Discovery of master's specialisations
Assessment
In-person semester exam (100%)
References:
The lecturer proposes references according to the chosen subject.
